Judgment on Moab


(Isaiah 15:1–9)

NoteThe Hebrew for Heshbon sounds like the Hebrew for plot.
NoteThe name of the Moabite town Madmen sounds like the Hebrew for silenced.
NoteHebrew; LXX her cries are heard as far away as Zoar
NoteOr like a wild donkey or like Aroer ’
NoteOr Give wings to Moab, for she would fly away
NoteJahzah is a variant of Jahaz; see verse 34.
NoteKir-heres is a variant of Kir-hareseth; also in verse 36; see Isaiah 16:7.
NoteProbably the Dead Sea
NoteTwo Hebrew manuscripts and LXX; most Hebrew manuscripts to the Sea of Jazer
NoteOr restore the fortunes of Moab ”
No metadata available for this word.

Judgment on the Ammonites

NoteOr their king; Milcom is a variant of Molech; also in verse 3; see Leviticus 18:21 and 1 Kings 11:7.
